question: Captures a standard that is being complied with.
keywords: standards compliance ISO IEEE ETSI regulations
summary: Captures a standard that is being complied with.
description:
A free-form text that captures a standard that the AI software complies with.

This includes both published and unpublished standards, such as those developed
by ISO, IEEE, and ETSI.

The standard may, but is not necessarily required to, satisfy a legal or
regulatory requirement.

retrieval.hypothetical_passage: This release is aligned with ISO/IEC 23053:2022 framework for AI systems and follows the data quality guidance in ISO/IEC 5259. The development process is audited against IEEE 7000-2021 for ethically aligned design. We also map our documentation to the NIST AI RMF 1.0 and the EU AI Act's transparency obligations. No formal certification has been obtained; compliance claims reflect internal self-assessment.

extraction.instruction: Extract the standards, regulations, or frameworks that the AI software complies with, naming each by its identifier (e.g. ISO/IEC 23053, IEEE 7000, NIST AI RMF, GDPR).
extraction.field_spec: SPDX property `ai_standardCompliance`. Free-form text (xsd:string), typically a comma-separated list of standard identifiers. Includes published (ISO, IEEE, ETSI, NIST) and unpublished standards; legal/regulatory frameworks may be included if treated as compliance targets.
extraction.output_guidance: List each standard as cited (with version/year if given), comma-separated. Distinguish certified compliance from self-asserted alignment when the source does. If the source mentions a regulation only as context (not as a compliance target), exclude it. Return noAssertion when standards are referenced but no compliance claim is made, or when none are mentioned.
